mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal broad economic strength and robust corporate revenue growth, leading to higher expected earnings and positive equity returns. This is a direct, always-on measure of economic momentum.
failure_condition: Periods of high monthly PAYEMS growth (e.g., >150k) are not followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 55% of the time over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the headline consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) level rises above a historically significant threshold, it signals persistent inflation that forces monetary tightening, increases discount rates, and compresses corporate margins, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500 produces positive returns over the subsequent 60 trading days in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence between strong consumer spending (RSAFS) and weakening housing investment (HOUST) signals an unsustainable, late-cycle demand profile. The decline in long-duration housing investment foreshadows a broader economic slowdown that is not yet reflected in current consumption, predicting negative returns.
failure_condition: The rank Information Coefficient of the combined signal does not exceed the IC of either RSAFS or HOUST individually over a 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: In a mid-to-late cycle expansion, a very low unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals maximum labor market tightness. This leads to accelerating wage growth, which compresses corporate profit margins and prompts the central bank to maintain tight policy, causing negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Periods where UNRATE is below 4.5% are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns on average.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a contraction in the manufacturing and goods-producing sectors of the economy. This is a leading indicator of broader economic weakness and declining corporate earnings, predicting negative equity market performance.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 fails to show statistically significant negative returns (p > 0.10) in the 20 trading days following a trigger event.

mechanism_narrative: A rising Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) increases the risk-free rate used to discount future corporate cash flows. This mechanical repricing of equity valuations leads to negative short-term returns as the market adjusts to higher rates.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a >25bps rate hike over 3 months is non-negative over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Strong growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects a robust and expanding economy. This translates directly into higher aggregate corporate earnings and improved investor sentiment, driving positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The correlation between quarterly real GDP growth and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is not statistically positive (p > 0.10) over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) and high inflation (CPIAUCSL) creates a stagflationary environment. This constrains the central bank from easing policy to support growth, leading to a severe contraction in earnings multiples and deeply negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward testing shows the interaction term in a regression adds no significant explanatory power (p > 0.10) over the individual UNRATE and CPIAUCSL signals.
